youtube_items.title: [YT] Does cleaner air make the planet hotter?
youtube_items.summary: [music] This is Climate Watch. [music] I'm Jaoyang. Welcome to the show. [music] China has seen a massive rapid cleanup of its skies over the past decade. By slashing coal use and upgrading power plants, the country has cleared the smog that once defined many of its cities, significantly improving public health. But lately, a startling counternarrative has emerged in the headlines. An article in The Economist argued that China's air quality improvements have hastened global warming. Meanwhile, research cited by Australia's ABC News suggests the cleanup may even be driving hotter and drier conditions in Australia.
